---
title: "Alex Knorre"
lightbox: false
page-layout: article

---

```{=html}
<div>
<img src="files/knorre_headshot.jpg" style="float:left;margin:0px 20px 0px 0px;    max-height:40%;
    max-width:40%;
    height:auto;
    width:auto;">
</div>
```

I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Criminology at [the University of South Florida](https://www.usf.edu/cbcs/criminology/faculty-staff/a-knorre.aspx). I received my Ph.D. in Criminology (2023) and A.M. in Statistics (2022) from [the University of Pennsylvania](https://crim.sas.upenn.edu), and then worked as a Postdoctoral Research Fellow at [Boston College](https://www.bc.edu/content/bc-web/schools/morrissey/departments/computer-science.html). Before joining Ph.D. program, I studied Russian criminal justice as a researcher with the Institute for the Rule of Law, European University in St Petersburg. 

My work focuses on victimization, gun violence, illegal drugs, and policy evaluation. I have recently published in PLoS One, Justice Quarterly, Journal of Criminal Justice, Aggression & Violent Behavior, and Criminology & Public Policy.


My CV is available here: [PDF, 120Kb](./files/Knorre_CV_Feb2026.pdf)

My profiles: [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexeyknorre/), [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre), [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=mR-1XBIAAAAJ&hl=en).

## Data

I prepared and maintain the following datasets:

Replication package for ``Unequal by the gun: Four Decades of Black-White Firearm Homicide Gap'' (2026) on [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre/racial_gap_gun_homicides)

Replication package for ''Measuring Changes in Bias-Motivated Attacks: Evidence from Anti-Asian Violence During the COVID-19 Pandemic'' (2025) on [OSF](https://osf.io/skv69/)

Replication package for ''Comparing risks of firearm-related death and injury among young adult males in US cities to wartime service in Iraq and Afghanistan'' (2022) on [Github](https://github.com/alexeyknorre/safer_at_war)

Russian Crime Victimization Survey, Wave I (2018) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/C2OTH9), Wave II (2021) on [Dataverse](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/SGRQTI)

Homicide rates in Russian cities (2020), data and guide in Russian on [Github](https://github.com/irlcode/russian_cities_homicide_data)

Also, check out [the TV Shows app](https://www.tvshowanalyzer.com).
